name: Kung Pao Pork
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Coarsely chopped green bell pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine the first 8 ingredients; stir until well-blended.
2. Set aside.
3. Trim excess fat from pork tenderloin and cut into 1-inch cubes.
4. In another bowl, combine the remaining ingredients (pork, cornstarch, soy sauce); stir well. Cover and refrigerate for 15 minutes.
5. Heat 1 teaspoon vegetable oil in a wok or large non-stick skillet over high heat. Add dried chiles; stir-fry for 1 minute until blackened. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
6. Add remaining oil to the pan, then add the marinated pork mixture. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
7. Add green bell pepper, onion, and gingerroot; stir-fry until vegetables are crisp-tender.
8. Add sherry mixture; stir-fry until thick and bubbly.
9. Remove from heat and stir in dried chiles and peanuts.
10. Serve over hot cooked rice.
